title: session.go OnDeliver callbacks: defer unlock + panic recovery
description:
File: internal/session/session.go, lines 493-497 (RunDaemon) and lines 595-599 (RunInteractive)

The OnDeliver closure locks the VT mutex, calls ForEachClient to render status bars, and unlocks manually. This closure runs inside the delivery goroutine (go s.StartServices() -> RunDelivery). RunDelivery has no recover.

Current risk: If RenderStatusBar() panics during delivery, the delivery goroutine dies (no more messages delivered to the agent), and the mutex stays locked (deadlocking the session). Two identical sites — one in RunDaemon, one in RunInteractive.

Fix:
1. Convert manual vt.Mu.Unlock() to defer-based unlock (wrap in anonymous func).
2. Add a recover() in the OnDeliver closure so a render panic doesn't kill the delivery goroutine — it should log the panic and return, allowing delivery to continue.
